title: Make test_browser_profile_coverage deterministic via DNS instrumentation
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

## Goal

Restructure `test_browser_profile_coverage` (in `test/test_crawl.py`) so it is deterministic regardless of network conditions. The current test asserts no unexpected domains appear in the browser profile, but `baidu.com` shows up intermittently when Firefox contacts search partners during initialization, causing flakes across all open PRs (tests group 1).

## Approach

Use the DNS instrumentation that landed in #1158 (redirect chain capture) and #1164 (failure capture via `onErrorOccurred`). For each site in `TEST_SITES`:

1. Assert a `dns_responses` row exists — proves the browser attempted resolution.
2. If `addresses IS NOT NULL` (DNS succeeded) → assert `http_requests` and `places.sqlite` entries exist for that site.
3. If DNS failed (`error IS NOT NULL`) → skip the HTTP / `places.sqlite` assertions for that domain.

A timeout/failure on a flaky live site (like `baidu.com`) becomes a valid test path, not a failure.

## References

- Upstream issue: https://github.com/openwpm/OpenWPM/issues/1163
- Comment with the revised approach: https://github.com/openwpm/OpenWPM/issues/1163#issuecomment-4209193720
- Prerequisites: #1158 (merged 2026-04-06), #1164 (merged 2026-04-16)

## Definition of done

- `test_browser_profile_coverage` is restructured to query `dns_responses` and gate HTTP / places assertions on DNS success.
- Test passes locally and in CI against `master`.
- Baidu (or any other intermittently-reachable site) no longer causes failures when DNS times out.
- A PR is opened against `openwpm/OpenWPM` master and linked back to GitHub issue #1163.
